title: odontites vernus subsp. salinus
intro: Odontites vernus subsp. salinus, also known as salt-leaved toothwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to create teas, salves, and ointments for topical and internal use.
